title: RipFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a Rip feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setAlongEdge
methods.description: Specifies the rip feature will be along an edge.

methods.name: setBetweenPoints
methods.description: This input method is for creating a rip between two points. Each point can be either a BRepVertex or a BRepEdge and an associated offset along the edge.

methods.name: setByFace
methods.description: Specifies the rip feature will be defined by a face..

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Rip Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sheet metal rip feature.

classType.title: RipFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setAlongEdge.title: RipFeatureInput.setAlongEdge Method
setAlongEdge.description: Specifies the rip feature will be along an edge.
setAlongEdge.return_description: Returns true if the defining the rip is successful.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.description: The BRepEdge that defines the location of the rip.

parameters.name: gapDistance
parameters.description: The gap distance of the rip.

setBetweenPoints.title: RipFeatureInput.setBetweenPoints Method
setBetweenPoints.description: This input method is for creating a rip between two points. Each point can be either a BRepVertex or a BRepEdge and an associated offset along the edge.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = ripFeatureInput_var.setBetweenPoints(pointOneEntity, pointTwoEntity, gapDistance)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = ripFeatureInput_var.setBetweenPoints(pointOneEntity, pointTwoEntity, gapDistance, pointOneOffset, pointTwoOffset)

setBetweenPoints.return_description: Returns true if the rip definition is successful.

parameters.name: pointOneEntity
parameters.description: The first point of the rip. This can be defined using a BrepVertex or a BRepEdge and offset to define where the point is along the edge. If an edge is specified, the pointOneOffset parameter must be specified.

parameters.name: pointTwoEntity
parameters.description: The second point of the rip and must lie on the same face as point 1. This can be defined using a BrepVertex or a BRepEdge and an offset to define where the point is along the edge. If an edge is specified, the pointTwoOffset parameter must be specified.

parameters.name: gapDistance
parameters.description: The gap distance of the rip.

parameters.name: pointOneOffset
parameters.description: If the first point lies on an edge, then this is the offset along the edge which defines the point. This is the physical distance from the topological start of the edge. If the offset is negative or exceeds the edge length, the corresponding vertex of the edge will be used.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

parameters.name: pointTwoOffset
parameters.description: If the second point lies on an edge, then this is the offset along the edge which defines the point. This is the physical distance from the topological start of the edge. If the offset is negative or exceeds the edge length, the corresponding vertex of the edge will be used.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setByFace.title: RipFeatureInput.setByFace Method
setByFace.description: Specifies the rip feature will be defined by a face..
setByFace.return_description: Returns true if the defining the rip is successful.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: The sheet metal face that defines the rip.

isValid.title: RipFeatureInput.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ripFeatureInput_var.isValid

objectType.title: RipFeatureInput.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = ripFeatureInput_var.objectType
